stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of fuel level using a fuel gauge. The measurements have a mean of 180.7 % and a standard deviation of 19.0 %. However, calibration tests reveal that a fuel gauge systematically overestimates the true value by between 2.3% and 7.3%. Estimate the true fuel level and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 180.7 %. After correcting for the 4.8% bias: ≈ 172.4 %. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (2.3%-7.3%) and sampling variability: [159.9, 185.1]. Point estimate: 172.4 %.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
